Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.value: Androgen synthesis inhibitor (abiraterone acetate), corticosteroid (prednisone), and hormonal therapy (androgen-deprivation therapy)
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.reasoning: Treatment Arm 1 is described in the 'METHODS' section on page 1 as 'androgen-deprivation therapy plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily, given once daily as four 250-mg tablets) plus prednisone (5 mg daily)'. The 'BACKGROUND' section on page 1 and 2 identifies abiraterone acetate as a drug that 'blocks endogenous androgen synthesis' and 'inhibits cytochrome P-450c17', classifying it as an androgen synthesis inhibitor. Prednisone is a corticosteroid, and androgen-deprivation therapy is a form of hormonal therapy.

Treatment Class.value: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) combined with an androgen synthesis inhibitor (abiraterone acetate) and a corticosteroid (prednisone), or ADT combined with placebos.
Treatment Class.reasoning: The BACKGROUND section states that the study evaluated 'abiraterone acetate plus prednisone with androgen-deprivation therapy'. The METHODS section further details that patients were assigned to 'androgen-deprivation therapy plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily)... plus prednisone (5 mg daily)' (the abiraterone group) or 'androgen-deprivation therapy plus dual placebos' (the placebo group). This indicates the treatment class is a combination therapy involving ADT, an androgen synthesis inhibitor (abiraterone acetate), and a corticosteroid (prednisone), or ADT with placebos.
